title: devstuff agent tool catalog
description: Schema for agent_tools.yaml (bundled at src/dev_setup/agent_tools.yaml, user overrides at ~/.config/dev-setup/agent_tools.yaml). Defines the tools exposed to the local model by `devstuff agent`. Mirrors the validation in agent/catalog.py — keep both in sync; this file is documentation for editor tooling and is NOT enforced at runtime.

expose_functions.description: Auto-expose every `type: script` entry in functions.yaml as an agent tool named fn_<key>. shell-eval functions are always excluded: they exist to mutate the calling shell, which a subprocess tool call cannot do.

exclude_functions.description: functions.yaml keys to withhold from the agent even when expose_functions is true.

tools.description: Map of tool key -> tool definition. The key is the function name the model calls.

propertyNames.description: Lowercase letters, digits and underscores; must start with a letter. No hyphens — the key is used verbatim as a JSON-Schema function name.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in `/tools`. Defaults to the catalog key.

description.description: REQUIRED. Sent to the model verbatim — this is how it decides whether to call the tool. Say what the tool does and when to prefer it over another one.

impl.description: primitive: dispatches to a callable in agent/primitives.py, keyed by the tool key. catalog: bridges to the devstuff tool registry via `target`. function: runs a functions.yaml script entry via `target`.

target.description: Required for impl catalog (one of: list_tools, search_catalog, tool_info, install_tool) and impl function (a functions.yaml key). Must be omitted for impl primitive, where dispatch is by tool key.

mutating.description: True if the tool changes something. Mutating tools require confirmation before each call, showing the exact command or a unified diff. Read-only tools run silently.

params.description: Named parameters, converted into the JSON Schema sent to the model.

name.description: Parameter name. Passed to the implementation as a dict key.

type.description: JSON Schema type advertised to the model.

description.description: Sent to the model. Say what a valid value looks like — small models take the example literally.

required.description: If true, a call omitting this parameter is rejected and the error is returned to the model so it can retry.

default.description: Used when the model omits the parameter or sends null. Any JSON value.

enum.description: Restrict the parameter to a fixed set of values.
